refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying what is required without indicating how to achieve it. The FR is atomic as it addresses one clear functional requirement related to communication quality for teleoperation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet loss stays below the threshold
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and control functions could create coupling if not modularized
Dependency on network conditions may require clear interface definitions
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring from control actions, supporting modularity and independence. Clear interface boundaries between monitoring and control modules will help avoid coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately in real-time
Assumes control system can adjust transmission parameters effectively to reduce packet loss
Risk that network variability exceeds control system capability leading to failure to meet packet loss target
Safety policies may impose constraints on allowable control actions affecting system responsiveness
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interface boundaries between monitoring and control functions in the DP to further reduce coupling risk and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to meet performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and monitor link status.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belongs here; minimal diffusion as most network control is centralized.
Low cohesion; vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle functions, network control would diffuse heavily outside.
Moderate cohesion since teleoperation depends on network quality, but control logic is not core to teleoperation itself.
Poor fit; safety services focus on safety alerts, not network control, leading to high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP, as it centralizes network-related functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for real-time tasking data may cause minor interface complexity.
If network control logic grows to include vehicle-specific adaptations, some diffusion into Vehicle module may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss target, which aligns with the customer-defined packet loss limit in the FR.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Teleoperation for real-time tasking data may cause minor interface complexity.
If network control logic grows to include vehicle-specific adaptations, some diffusion into Vehicle module may occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion, centralizing network quality monitoring and control. Other modules show low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and adjusting transmission parameters through the Network Link interface to ensure packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces supporting verification and control functions.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage interface complexity with the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion risk.
Plan for potential future adaptations that may require coordination with the Vehicle module to avoid diffusion of network control logic.
Document interface contracts clearly to support verification of packet loss targets through Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical alignment, manageable diffusion risks, and feasible verification. The current decomposition level sufficiently addresses the high safety criticality by ensuring a centralized, high-cohesion implementation module with well-defined interfaces. Recommended revisions focus on interface management and documentation rather than further decomposition, indicating that the current level is appropriate and stable.

revise.why: To monitor and manage interface complexity with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules, and to document interface contracts clearly for verification purposes.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can maintain high cohesion and minimal diffusion as system complexity grows.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules can be managed to prevent significant diffusion.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation module.
Possible diffusion of network control logic into Vehicle module if future adaptations occur.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss metrics remain within customer-defined limits during operation.
Interface contract documentation confirming clear and testable boundaries.
